todos.description: Report non-convergence rate: count dialogues where majority_vote returned None (tie) and surface in report.py as a 6th data-prep helper + in final-report Methods
todos.notes: sp-feedback f-20260422-07. debate-runner optimization suggestion never acted on. One-line numpy count over dialogues corpus.

todos.description: Spot-check aggregator CLI: read filled artifacts/spot_check.csv and emit pass-rate + representative failures for IMRAD Methods section
todos.notes: sp-feedback f-20260422-08. Gated on human actually doing the spot-check; 20 lines of csv code; zero LLM calls.

todos.description: Replicate Gate-1 + Gate-2 null + control on at least one other model (gpt-4o-mini or Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct) to check whether 'debate is voting, not dialogue' is Qwen2.5-7B specific or a general property on GSM8K
todos.notes: If other models also show control-Δ=0 on single_wrong ∧ debate_right subset → strong finding. If at least one model shows Δ>0 → Qwen specific, possibly due to Qwen's strong independent solve. Cost: ~$1-3 for gpt-4o-mini full pipeline, free for Llama if on HPC.

todos.description: Audit + redesign debate prompts to check if Qwen's lack of correction/question behavior (0.3%, 0%) is prompt-induced. Try 'you MUST identify at least one disagreement' variants for skeptic, and 'ask one clarifying question' for synthesizer. Re-extract + re-ablate to see if claim distribution diversifies and ablation signal emerges
todos.notes: Complement to other-model test. If aggressive prompts produce more correction/question claims AND ablation Δ_t becomes non-zero → debate prompts were 'too cooperative'. If distribution shifts but Δ still 0 → voting hypothesis robust. Will reuse existing LLM cache for pilot.
